description: Hand-vectorizing a loop that is memory-bandwidth-bound, adding complexity and ISA lock-in for no speedup.
body: Failure mode: a loop is limited by DRAM bandwidth, but it gets rewritten with AVX intrinsics expecting a lane-width speedup; the cores were already waiting on memory, so SIMD does nothing while adding portability and maintenance cost. Detection signals: profile shows back-end/memory-bound stalls before the change; near-zero speedup after vectorizing; bandwidth counters near peak. Do not vectorize a memory-bound loop. Safer fallback: first fix locality (layout, blocking/tiling, prefetch) to cut traffic, re-profile, and only vectorize once the loop is compute-bound with a scalar fallback retained.
